dialogueChunks.intro: They're home, {player_name}. After everything, they're finally home.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When {target_npc} walked through the gates of {location}, the whole settlement stopped. People who'd given up hope were weeping in the streets. {target_npc} is thin and scarred, but their eyes are bright and their grip is still strong. They told stories by the fire last night: about the days in the dark, the fear, the stubborn refusal to give up hope. They said they kept going because they knew someone would come. And someone did.
dialogueChunks.outro: You brought back someone we'd already mourned. There's no payment that matches that, but take this and know that you'll never want for a warm fire in {location}.

playerResponses.text: Seeing them by the fire, alive and home. That's payment enough.
They survived because they didn't give up. The credit belongs to them.

dialogueChunks.intro: You found them, {player_name}. Alive, changed, but alive.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is recovering in the healer's care. The time alone in the wilderness changed them: they're quieter, more watchful, slower to trust the safety of walls. The healers say the physical wounds will mend, but the time in captivity and isolation left deeper marks. Still, when I sat beside them and held their hand, they squeezed back. The family hasn't stopped crying: happy tears, the kind that come after a long drought.
dialogueChunks.outro: Recovery will be slow, but it starts today because of you. Take this with the gratitude of every person who loves {target_npc}.

playerResponses.text: Time heals what medicine can't. I'm glad they're back where they belong.
The hard part is over. The rest is patience and kindness.

dialogueChunks.intro: You found the truth, {player_name}. It wasn't the answer I wanted, but it was the one I needed.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} didn't vanish because of the {enemy_type}. They left by choice: overwhelmed by obligations, by expectations, by the weight of being needed by too many people. You found them living quietly in a small camp {location_hint}, healthy and at peace for the first time in years. They asked me not to be angry, and I'm trying. They sent back {quest_item} and a letter explaining everything. It hurts, but at least I know they're alive and they made a choice rather than having one made for them.
dialogueChunks.outro: The answer wasn't kidnapping or tragedy. It was just a person who needed to breathe. Take this, {player_name}. The truth was worth more than any rescue.

playerResponses.text: Sometimes finding someone means accepting they chose to leave. I'm sorry.
At least you know they're safe. That's not nothing.
